name: Vía Verde de la Sierra Trail
name_original: Vía Verde de la Sierra por el Río Guadalporcún — ida y vuelta por la Reserva Natural  Peñón de Zaframagón
description: Hike the moderate 8.0-mile Vía Verde de la Sierra Trail through the Reserva Natural Peñón de Zaframagón, featuring railway tunnels and griff
region_intro: Hiking around Coripe, nestled in the Sierra de Algodonales, offers diverse landscapes for outdoor activities. The region features a mountainous terrain with hills, valleys, traditional orchards, and olive groves. A significant natural feature is the Vía Verde de la Sierra, an old railway line transformed into a 36-kilometer route, alongside the impressive Peñón de Zaframagón Nature Reserve. Best hiking trails around Coripe The most popular hiking route is Via Verde del Segura — round trip through the Zaframagon Rock Nature Reserve, a 7.8 miles (12.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 25 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the natural reserve, home to a large griffon vulture colony. Another top favourite among local hikers is Vía Verde de la Sierra Trail, a moderate 8.0 miles (12.9 km) path. This trail follows an old railway line, featuring tunnels and viaducts through varied natural scenery. Local hikers also love the Sierra Greenway Trail, an 8.1 miles (13.0 km) trail leading through the Vía Verde de la Sierra, often completed in about 3 hours 32 minutes. Hiking around Coripe is defined by the Vía Verde de la Sierra, the Peñón de Zaframagón Nature Reserve, and the El Estrechón Canyon. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging trails. The routes in Coripe are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Coripe's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Coripe?
A: Coripe offers a wide selection of hiking opportunities, with over 45 trails to explore. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy strolls to more challenging treks through the mountainous landscape.

Q: What kind of terrain will I encounter on hikes in Coripe?
A: The terrain around Coripe is diverse, featuring a blend of hills, valleys, traditional orchards, and olive groves. Many trails follow the historic Vía Verde de la Sierra, an old railway line, which means you'll encounter unique features like tunnels and viaducts. The region also includes impressive rocky formations and river canyons.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Coripe?
A: Yes, Coripe offers several circular hiking options. For a more challenging experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009546343/sierra-de-lijar-circular-from-la-muela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Líjar — circular from La Muela&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 24 km. There's also a moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1274599" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Mirador de Levante&lt;/a&gt;, perfect for a shorter outing.

Q: What unique natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Coripe?
A: Coripe is rich in natural and historical landmarks. You can explore the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3219631" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón de Zaframagón Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, home to a large colony of griffon vultures. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3358115" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Chaparro de la Vega&lt;/a&gt;, a centennial holm oak, is another protected natural monument. Additionally, the Vía Verde de la Sierra features stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/769364" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;tunnels and viaducts&lt;/a&gt;, offering unique views of the landscape, including the El Estrechón Canyon.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Coripe?
A: Many sections of the Vía Verde de la Sierra are suitable for families, offering relatively flat and well-maintained paths. These routes provide a safe and enjoyable way to experience the region's nature and historical railway infrastructure. The recreational area around El Chaparro de la Vega also offers easier trails.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Coripe?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many of the trails around Coripe, especially along the Vía Verde de la Sierra. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, particularly in nature reserves like the Peñón de Zaframagón, to protect local wildlife, especially the griffon vultures.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Coripe?
A: The best time to hike in Coripe is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder and the landscape is vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable. Winters are generally mild, but some trails might be wet after rain.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the hiking trails in Coripe?
A: Yes, Coripe itself offers amenities, including places to eat and stay. The former railway station, now the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3221171" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hostal Restaurante Estación de Coripe&lt;/a&gt;, is a notable spot right on the Vía Verde. You'll find local cafes and restaurants in the town to refuel after your hike.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Coripe?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 90 reviews. Reviewers often praise the unique experience of hiking along the Vía Verde de la Sierra, the stunning views of the Peñón de Zaframagón, and the chance to spot griffon vultures.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the hiking areas in Coripe?
A: While Coripe is accessible by road, public transport options might be limited. It's recommended to check local bus schedules if you plan to rely on public transport. Many visitors prefer to drive to the area, as it offers more flexibility for accessing various trailheads.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the trails in Coripe?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain and the Vía Verde de la Sierra offer several excellent viewpoints. The old railway infrastructure, particularly the viaducts, provides privileged views into deep gorges like the El Estrechón Canyon. There's also a specific &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2721734" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View point&lt;/a&gt; highlighted in the area, offering panoramic vistas.
tips: The Vía Verde de la Sierra runs along the old Jerez-Almargen railway line. At present, 36.5 km are passable. joining the towns of Puerto Serrano and Olvera. Along this route you can enjoy a great variety of landscapes, observing the transition between an eminently agricultural landscape to another mountain, alternating with masses of Mediterranean forest (holm oaks, gall oaks, carob trees, mastic, olive trees, etc.)

The Guadalporcún River rises in the Sierra Norte of the province of Cádiz in the municipality of Alcalá del Valle, and flows into the Guadalete River in the vicinity of Puerto Serrano. The river is not regulated, so the flow regime is natural.

faqs.question: Where can I access the Vía Verde de la Sierra Trail and find parking?
faqs.answer: The Vía Verde de la Sierra connects the towns of Puerto Serrano and Olvera. You can access the trail from either end, or from various points in between. Former railway stations along the route have often been converted and can serve as good access points with parking. Specific parking details would depend on your chosen starting point.

faqs.question: Is the Vía Verde de la Sierra Trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally considered suitable for beginners and families. Built on a former railway line, it features a relatively flat and smooth path with gentle inclines. While some sections have slopes varying from 3% to 9%, it's accessible to a wide range of abilities, including people with reduced mobility.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily consists of compacted gravel, making for a smooth walking surface. You'll also encounter some sections of dirt and paved roads. The gentle gradients, due to its railway heritage, mean there are no steep climbs or descents.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Vía Verde de la Sierra Trail?
faqs.answer: Highlights include the historical railway infrastructure with 30 tunnels (the longest being Coripe Mountain at 990 meters, which is lit) and 4 impressive viaducts, such as the Bridge Over the Guadalporcún River. You'll also pass through the Vía Verde de la Sierra itself, the Peñón de Zaframagón nature reserve, and the ancient "Chaparro de la Vega" holm oak tree.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I see on the trail, especially in the Peñón de Zaframagón reserve?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through the Sierra de Grazalema Natural Park and the Peñón de Zaframagón nature reserve, which is home to one of Europe's largest colonies of griffon vultures. Bird-watching hides are available near the reserve for observation. You can expect to see a variety of other birds and local fauna in this biodiverse area.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike the Vía Verde de la Sierra Trail?
faqs.answer: The full length of the Vía Verde de la Sierra is approximately 36 to 38 kilometers (22 to 24 miles). While the Komoot tour for a section is around 12.9 km and takes about 3.5 hours, hiking the entire trail would typically take a full day or could be split into multiple days, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Are there facilities like water, cafes, or accommodations along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is well-equipped with amenities. You'll find water supply points, information panels, signs, and rest areas. Several former railway stations along the route have been converted into accommodations or cafes, offering convenient stops for refreshments and overnight stays.

faqs.question: Does the Vía Verde de la Sierra Trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is part of and intersects with other official trails. Notably, it covers 100% of the Via Serrana route in sections. Other parts of the Vía Verde de la Sierra also intersect with trails like Túnel de Zamarra, Túnel del Atajo, Túnel de la alberquilla, and Túnel del Testarudo.

faqs.question: Is the Vía Verde de la Sierra Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on Vías Verdes in Spain, but it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas or nature reserves like Peñón de Zaframagón, to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Vía Verde de la Sierra Trail?
faqs.answer: The best seasons to hike are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these times, the weather is milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities. Summers can be very hot in Andalusia, and winters can be cooler and wetter, though often still suitable for hiking on clear days.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the trail or the Peñón de Zaframagón nature reserve?
faqs.answer: There are no general entrance fees or permits required to hike the Vía Verde de la Sierra itself. The Peñón de Zaframagón is a nature reserve, and while access to the trail through it is free, specific regulations regarding wildlife observation (e.g., using bird hides) or activities might apply. Always respect local signage and conservation rules.
